response:
The fare equals ten plus two times the distance minus four when the distance exceeds four.
This rule applies to every trip that goes beyond the initial four kilometer limit.
We calculate the extra cost by taking the distance and subtracting the base four.
Spring brings many travelers to the city and they often take taxis for rides.
The driver must round up any fraction of a kilometer to the next whole number.

A fare of thirty eight yuan implies a total distance of eighteen kilometers was charged.
Since the actual drive was fifteen kilometers the extra three kilometers came from waiting time.
Each five minutes of waiting adds one kilometer to the calculated distance for billing.
Therefore the maximum waiting time is fifteen minutes because three times five equals fifteen.
Spring weather often causes traffic delays that force drivers to wait in long lines.
